generators.description: Create an analytics provider wrapper component
Create a typed analytics event tracking function

patterns.name: Typed Events
patterns.pattern: type AnalyticsEvent = | { name: 'page_view'; path: string } | { name: 'button_click'; label: string }
patterns.description: Define a discriminated union for all analytics events to ensure type safety

patterns.name: Track Helper
patterns.pattern: export function track(event: AnalyticsEvent) { window.gtag?.('event', event.name, event) }
patterns.description: Centralise all tracking calls through a single typed function

patterns.name: Page View on Route Change
patterns.description: Fire page_view events automatically on route transitions

patterns.name: Consent Guard
patterns.description: Always check for user consent before sending analytics data (GDPR)

integrations.description: PostHog product analytics — open source, self-hostable
steps: Initialize PostHog with your project API key
Enable session recording and feature flags as needed

integrations.description: Vercel Web Analytics — zero-config page view tracking
steps: Deploy to Vercel — page views tracked automatically
Use track() from @vercel/analytics for custom events

integrations.install_command: # Add script tag to index.html (no npm package required)
steps: Add the GA4 script tag to index.html with your Measurement ID
Create a typed track() wrapper in src/lib/analytics.ts
Gate tracking behind user consent to comply with GDPR

questions.answer: Before collecting analytics, obtain explicit user consent (required in EU/UK). Implement a consent banner and gate all tracking calls behind a consent check. PostHog and Plausible offer cookie-free, privacy-respecting options.
Define a typed AnalyticsEvent discriminated union, then funnel all calls through a single track() function. This prevents typos in event names and makes refactoring safe.
SPAs don't trigger full page loads, so fire page_view manually on route changes. Listen to navigation events from your router (TanStack Router: useRouterState) and call track() in a useEffect.
